entry.title: Re: Adobe Forms CheckBox on Condition
entry.content:
Hi Gianni,

Create a hidden field named QMART that is bound to VIQMEL-QMART inside subform BOX_GREY.  Set the checkboxes so they are unbound and calculated, and use the following Javascript for each field, and of course the string value would change for each checkbox.

this.rawValue = this.parent.QMART.rawValue == "Z1" ? 1 : 0;

 

Regards,

Ryan Crosby

entry.title: Read Rate plan (with pricing scheme) data / charges via API
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I need to read data of rate plans set up in our Subscription billing system -  the most important information i need to retrieve is the charge(s) assigned to the rate plans.
I tested following services both from the Try out page of the Acclerator Hub, and via Postman:

- Basic Rate Plan Functions :
/rate-plans (GET)
/rate-plans{ratePlanId} (GET)

- Rate Plan View
/rate-plans/{ratePlanId}/view (GET)
/rate-plans/view/$batch (request = get in the body)

In all the cases, the only data retrieved is the following:

"id": "019179ef-2fad-43f8-bb6b-e5ec81f00555",
    "description": "Abonnement non forfaitaire, 4 rate elements, no subelements",
    "periodicity": "calendar-monthly",
    "endOfMonthRule": "billFromCurrentCycle",
    "currency": "EUR",
    "billingProfileId": "_initial",
    "snapshots": [
        {}
    ],
    "marketId": "BE00"
 
How can I get the full set of data?
In the response of tests executed in the Accelerator hub Sandbox, only rate plans  that do NOT refer to pricing schemes are retrieved.
On the other hand, all our rate plans DO refer to pricing schemes.
Is this the reason why only incomplete data are retrieved? If so, what's the solution?

Thank you in advance for your help.
 
Kind Regards,
Elena

entry.title: Professionelles Change Management mit dem SAP Activate Roadmap Viewer
entry.content:
Die Einführung einer SAP Cloud-Lösung ist mehr als ein reines IT-Projekt, sie ist eine ganzheitliche Businesstransformation. Was heißt das konkret? Unternehmen profitieren von neuen Best Practice-Prozessen, der Automatisierung von Routineaufgaben, einer schnellen Verfügbarkeit von Daten und einer hohen Transparenz von Informationen. Für die betroffenen Mitarbeiterinnen und Mitarbeiter bedeutet das, sich von liebgewonnenen Gewohnheiten zu verabschieden und eine neue Art der Zusammenarbeit zu leben. Dieser Prozess läuft umso reibungsloser, je besser die Belegschaft dabei unterstützt, informiert und auf die neue Situation vorbereitet und qualifiziert wird.

Der Mehrwert eines projektbegleitenden Change Managements ist deshalb unstrittig. Aber welche konkreten Aktivitäten sind zu welchem Zeitpunkt notwendig, um die sogenannte “softe” Seite einer Cloud-Einführung professionell abzudecken? Und was ist bei der Planung und Umsetzung von Maßnahmen zu beachten?

Für alle Projekte, die der bewährten SAP-Activate Implementierungsmethodologie folgen, bietet der Roadmap Viewer von SAP praktische Antworten auf diese Fragen. Dort ist das Thema „Organizational Change Management“ seit kurzem als Querschnittsthema integriert. Interessierte finden eine kompakte Darstellung des Change Management-Ansatzes der SAP mit seinen sechs Dimensionen und den zugehörigen Arbeitspaketen (sogenannte „Deliverables“) für die einzelnen Projektphasen. Die folgende Abbildung zeigt die Deliverables, sortiert nach Activate-Phasen, im Überblick:

Alle Deliverables werden detailliert beschrieben: Eine einführende Erläuterung gibt einen Überblick, und klar strukturierte Umsetzungsschritte zeigen auf, wie die Implementierung in der Projektpraxis erfolgt. Zusätzliche Unterstützung bieten praktische Tools und Templates, die heruntergeladen und auf den jeweiligen Projektkontext angepasst werden können.

Interessiert? Alle Informationen zu Change Management finden Sie kostenlos unter diesem Link. Für den Zugriff auf den Roadmap Viewer ist lediglich eine Registrierung (Universal ID für SAP-Inhalte) erforderlich. Viel Erfolg bei der Change Management-Begleitung Ihrer Cloud-Implementierung!

Und noch ein Hinweis: Wenn Sie sich für weitere Angebote rund um das Thema Change Management interessieren, besuchen Sie unsere Web-Site Organizational Change Management mit SAP.

entry.title: Re: Unable to delete the released work order
entry.content:
Hi,

The deletion flag cannot be set for a work order with release status.

You can first set the status of the work order to TECO->CLSD by first technically completing and then business completing the work order.

If this works for a single order in your case, then you can set the work orders to TECO and CLSD in mass using standard report/transactions.

Regards,

Vijay

entry.title: Error - Actual Assessment is replaced by Universal Allocation
entry.content:
When trying to access the App - 'Create Overhead Allocation Cycles - Cost Centers - Actual' (KSU1) the following error is returned - 

Actual Assessment is replaced by Universal Allocation. See text.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Error - Actual Assessment is replaced by Universal Al...
entry.content:
This is detailed in the KBA - 

3486075 - Deprecation of Apps "Overhead Allocation Cycles - Cost Centers – Actual"(KSU*) - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Cause

The information about deprecation means the above apps (KSU*) still work and are officially supported, but it is not recommended to use them anymore. No improvements / new features can be expected. 

Resolution

The above apps (KSU*) are blocked for new customers, which have not been using the apps previously. All existing customers which have been using the apps, will be able to continue using the apps. 

The functionality of this app will be replaced by Universal Allocation. If you are not yet using this app productively, consider switching to the Universal Allocation apps.

entry.title: Designing with Code - Update to Version 2 of UI5 Web Components
entry.content:
Designing with Code – Why Should I?

In my article series Designing with Code, I shared my experience of spending six months working within a product design team. By stepping out of my comfort zone—my beloved design tool—and diving into creating first prototypes using UI5 web components, I found myself much more connected to the actual end result. Using my entry-level coding skills, I gained insights into what happens behind the pixels, which greatly improved my discussions with developers.

In subsequent articles, I introduced how designers can leverage web components, CSS, JavaScript, and HTML to create interactive prototypes. This hands-on approach not only deepens your understanding of components but also allows you to experience how they behave in real-world scenarios.

To clarify: I am not a front-end developer. But just as a basic understanding of design benefits developers, designers can work more efficiently by grasping the basics of coding. This is particularly helpful when exploring how components interact or how screen readers interpret your designs. For me, it was a true eye-opener.

I hope this inspires you to get started! You might want to revisit Part 3, where I cover a simple prototyping setup, or Part 4, which introduces the model-view-controller pattern.

An Update: Why Some Examples No Longer Work

Unfortunately, the examples in my previous articles are now outdated due to two major changes:

  • Updated UI5 Documentation: The UI5 team migrated their documentation to a new system, integrating a sleek playground feature. This allows you to view live example code, make modifications, and preview results directly in the browser. If you haven’t tried it yet, I highly recommend it! However, this migration rendered the old links to the UI5 web components library invalid, breaking the examples in my articles.
  • Release of UI5 Web Components Version 2: A new version of UI5 web components introduced several improvements, including refactoring and stabilization, as well as exciting new components.

Given these changes, I’ve updated the examples in the GitHub repository to align with the latest version:

GitHub Repository – Designing with Code

Disclaimer: The prototyping setup described here is not intended for production use. For building production-grade applications, please refer to the official UI5 documentation.

Simple Prototyping Setup

Building production-ready applications often involves complex tools like build systems, testing frameworks, and dependency management. For designers, this can be overwhelming and time-consuming. Thankfully, there are simpler alternatives for prototyping:

Playground

The UI5 playground allows you to experiment with components by editing code and seeing instant results. This is sufficient for most exploratory tasks. However, the playground doesn’t allow for permanent saving or advanced interactions.

Local Prototyping Setup

For more complex prototypes or continuous work, a local setup offers greater flexibility. Below, I outline a minimal installation and configuration process that avoids heavy tools while providing key advantages:

  • No need to manage tools or packages locally.
  • No build steps—simply refresh your browser.
  • Easy sharing of prototypes.

Setting Up the Prototype: Key Changes in the New Approach

Import Map vs. Bundle

Previously, we imported a single bundle that included all libraries, making every component available but at a performance cost. The new approach uses an import map, which loads only the components you explicitly reference, improving efficiency and compatibility with real deployments.

To include a component, locate the <script> tag in the UI5 playground under the expanded code header. Copy and paste it into your HTML file.

I recommend using the sources available on the GitHub repository to get started. These files might also serve you as basis for your own projects going forward. The previous examples are still available. I have added an updated version in parallel folders:

  • Old Setup: Part-03-SAP-Web-Components/01_button
  • New Setup: Part-03-SAP-Web-Components 2.0/01_button

Manual Imports

With the new setup, you must manually import each required component. Create a main.js file in the same folder as your index.html and add the relevant imports. For example, to use a button component:

 

 

import "@ui5/webcomponents/dist/Button.js";

 

 

If a component doesn’t render as expected, double-check your imports.

The import statement, that is needed to import a component can be found in the documentation under the section “ES6 Module Import”. Figure 3 shows the import statement in the documentation for the Avatar component.

Running the Prototype: HTTP Server Required

Modern browsers require HTTP servers to dynamically load resources. You can use:

Visual Studio Code with Live Preview

Install the Live Preview extension for an integrated HTTP server in Visual Studio Code.

(Recommended for simplicity)

Node.js and http-server

http-server is a lightweight alternative for more control over your setup.

Is It Worth the Effort?

Admittedly, this new setup requires more effort compared to the old bundle-based approach. However, it offers significant advantages:

  1. Lightweight and Portable: Avoid managing local installations while benefiting from version control. Share your prototypes easily using platforms like GitHub Pages.
  2. Realistic Testing: Standalone prototypes allow you to test interactions like keyboard behavior, screen reader support, and resizing without playground constraints.
  3. Developer-Ready Code: The new setup aligns more closely with production code, enabling developers to reuse your prototypes effectively.

For quick experiments, the playground remains the fastest option. But for long-term projects or detailed explorations, this setup strikes a great balance between simplicity and functionality.

entry.title: 401k/Roth combined employer match issue
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

We have an issue when we match a  combined contribution of a 401(k) and a Roth plan

401(k) and Roth = 100% match on the first 3% and then 50% match on the next 3% which equates to a MAX of 4.50% employer match.

Scenario:

Employee elects the two at:

3% 401(k) = 3% Employer Match

3% Roth = 3% Employer Match

SAP calculates a total of 6% employer match which exceeds the 4.50% employer match.

Has anyone else experienced this and if so, what have you done to resolve this?

entry.title: Get ready to discover hidden opportunities with Community Demand Trends on SAP Business Network
entry.content:
What if you could watch market trends and fluctuations in demand as they happen? On January 1st, 2025, all Platinum suppliers on SAP Business Network will have this capability, with the release of Community Demand Trends. 

Community Demand Trends is a powerful tool for any supplying organization, allowing them to react fast to any change and identify previously hidden sales opportunities by spotting trends and variations in demand in near-real-time fashion. This provides the insights and analysis they need to make fact-based strategic decisions in a timely manner and keep their competitiveness as suppliers.

A new way to leverage SAP Business Network
Evolving way beyond being a simple transactional platform, SAP Business Network will offer commerce data worth 5 trillion $ to the lens of the suppliers. Channel manager or sales individual will use Demand Trends to evaluate buying behaviors of their own customer base, explore demand trends across the entire network (e.g. the top growing commodities), and check their performance relative to all transactions of trading partners on SAP Business Network.

A unique capability in many ways
Community Demand Trends is unique in width, with suppliers accessing an ecosystem representing more than 5 trillion US$ of spend. The data consists of anonymized PO data and is enriched by machine learning.
It is also unique in its granularity, offering filters at the commodity level. Suppliers can explore demand by UNSPSC segment, family, and class, as well as filter by parent or child account, customer, region or time range.

Here's how to get ready
Here are a few things you can do prior to January 1st, 2025, to ensure that you be able to use Community Demand Trends:

  • Check that your account is at the Platinum subscription level,
  • Inform sales managers, channel managers, analysts and other colleagues from your organization that might benefit from these unique insights,
  • As an account administrator, create relevant users and ensure they have the permission "Analytics Dashboard Access" enabled.

Once released, you'll be able to access Demand Trends from the Reports tab. 

Here's to a successful year 2025!

The solution:

 

cds.foreach(cds.parse.cql("SELECT ID FROM db_table"), (each: table) => console.log(each.ID))

entry.title: Re: Adopting General Display Name
entry.content:
Hello,

I just wanted to know if it is possible to remove the Display name column on the User data file if the adoption of the General display name is disabled?

I have checked this kba: 3261324 - General Display Name Troubleshooting - SAP for Me

It mentioned here that The DISPLAYNAME Column will be added to the UDF for all instances as part of the 2H 2022 Release update. 
Removing the DISPLAYNAME completely in the UDF or in the system can't be done according to design. Although display name adoption is an Admin Opt-in, it only controls the whole BizX system applied with display name in all pages. The display name field will always be added to all users including UDF even if you don't use it.

So I assume that it is not possible since it is by system design that the Display name column cannot be removed?

entry.title: SAP PaPM Cloud Universal Model: What’s New as of 2024-11-19?
entry.content:
Hello, my fellow SAP enthusiasts! Welcome to our latest blogpost on SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud – Universal Model (SAP PaPM Cloud UM)! 

In the fast-evolving world of business process management, staying ahead of the curve is essential. SAP PaPM Cloud – UM continues to innovate and expand, offering powerful new features that drive smarter decision-making and optimize performance. In this blogpost, we’ll dive into the latest updates to the Universal Model in SAP PaPM Cloud – what’s new, what’s improved, and how these advancements can help organizations streamline their processes and achieve better outcomes. Whether you're already using SAP PaPM Cloud UM or just exploring its capabilities, this blog will give you an overview of the key features to look out for. Let's explore how these updates can elevate your financial and operational strategies! 

If you want to catch up the previous blog posts from my colleague, you can always go through and read them all using this tag PaPMCloudUMWhatsNew. 

Let’s now dive into those exciting new features!

GENERAL  

Copy Functionality Enhancement  

When copying an object entity such as Environment, Field, Function, Activity, Process, Report, or Pages, the original object remains selected. With the new enhancement, after a single object is copied, the system automatically opens and redirects to the General Information screen of the first copied entity and start configuring or enhancing it.  

Is Protected Attribute for Process and Activities, Teams, Layouts, Reports and Pages  

In SAP PaPM Cloud UM, environments and its elements can be protected using the “Set Protection” functionality. For further information about this, you may refer to the Application Help for Universal Model Manage Environments.  

Users can see if an environment is protected or not with the read-only attribute Is Protected in its General Information section.  

This is also true for Process and Activities.  

 

In relation to this, for Teams, Layouts, Reports and Pages, “Is Protected” attribute is now visible and its value depends on whether the Runtime Environment is protected or not. 

“No” if its Runtime Environment Is Protected = “No”  
“Yes” if its Runtime Environment Is Protected = “Yes”  

  

  

  

 
MODEL 

Manage Functions: Diagram  

APIs can exist in one of five states: Active, Beta, Decommissioned, Deprecated, or Internal—each marking a different stage in the API’s lifecycle. No need to worry about the state of a function—you can easily check it in Diagrams, where each node displays icons representing the current API state.  

When Function is in Internal state, you will see ‘I’ and upon hovering icon it will display which state it is:   

  

When API state is Active, there should be green check mark in left corner of function.  

  

When API state is Beta, there should be blue letter B in left corner of function.  

  

When API state is Decommissioned, there should be red X in left corner of function.  

 

When API state is Deprecated, there should be orange triangle in left corner of function.  

 

For more information about API states, you can refer to SAP Help Portal: Change API State of Function | SAP Help Portal and APIs and Events Deprecation Policy | SAP Help Portal.  

Add Join Conditions for Unmapped Relations via OData Service Connection type 

This is a new enhancement to Manage Connections with Target Type = OData Service that gives users more flexibility with unmatched relations. In the past, relations without a predefined join condition were hidden from the list during synchronization, limiting their usability. With the new enhancement, these relations will appear in the list, allowing users to manually define a join condition and enable syncing for these previously unavailable relations.  

Upon synchronization, some relations will not have join conditions, user will see information message that models without join condition in relation was excluded.   

 

Relation without join condition will be visible in relation section of the Model, with “Sync Relation” = Remove  

 

User can manually add join conditions for these relations, enabling sync functionality even if metadata doesn’t provide a condition by default.  

  

Upon saving the configuration, re-synchronization of models and re-activation of environment, relations will be added to corresponding model entity.   

 

Validation Checks for View and Model View

We’ve introduced validation checks to ensure that all mandatory fields and dropdown selections are correctly populated in Model View and View functions. Key validation features include:  

Validation for View Functions   

  • Obligatory Fields Check: Ensures that all required fields in both the Header (General Information) and Fields sections are filled in. If any required field is left empty, an error message stating “Value is required” will be displayed.   
  • Dropdown Selections Validation: Validates that proper items or tables are selected from dropdown menus. Incorrect selections trigger specific error messages related to the selected fields.   

Example of validation messages:  

When field is left empty in View Function General Information section

   

When field mapping has duplicates in View Function  

 The same validation checks are also implemented in Model View function.  

Manage Model Calculation UI incorporates Intellisense 

The newly implemented feature is about maintaining and editing of formula on the Assignment section of the Model Calculation Function.    

On the assignment section we have introduced the Edit button.  Choosing this button, a pop-up screen will appear where the user will see a template. It was enhanced so that whenever a user adds a character to the formula box a predictive text appears thereby assisting the user to edit/configure the formula. 

PROCESS 

‘Copy to’ Functionality in Manage Process  

Building complex processes with numerous activities and links can be time-consuming. To streamline this, the SAP PaPM Cloud UM now offers the 'Copy To' option in Manage Processes, allowing users to copy entire process, including all activities and links, from one environment to another.  

Copy To functionality for Process in Protected Environment would work as well.  

For better tracking of copied processes to another environment, you can check the 'copyProcessestoEnv' log in the Event Logs.  

  

Navigate to the Manage Activity object page from Activity Show Screen  

If you need quick navigation to Manage Activity object page from Show Screen, you can use additional menu item that we added in dropdown.  

Clicking the Activity menu item will redirect you to the Manage Activity page. This Activity menu text is composed of <activity.description> - Activity.  

  

Activity function Value Help 

In Manage Activity, when the user is defining an Activity Function using the Value help, an additional column is added to give additional information to the users what is the API State of the available function that can be used in the Activity. 

 

Using of activity layout for data export   

A new enhancement has been introduced in Manage Activity for exporting of data wherein it will apply the set default layout for the activity in the exported data file.  

Note:  Exporting of data can be exported into a CSV file or XLXS file.  
The exported data can be downloaded from the Manage Comments application.  

What are the layouts that will be applied during exporting of data?  

Exporting Data with Show Screen Layout in General Information  

First, if the activity has a defined layout in Show Screen Layout field in General information, like the image below:

  

In the exported data file, the layout used in the activity, like in the image below:  

  

will be the layout applied in the exported data file.  

  

Exporting Data with default non-standard layout in Show Data screen  

Second, if the activity has a defined default layout in Show Data screen, like the image below  

  

  

  

In the exported data file, the default layout used in the activity show data screen, will be the layout applied in the file without the need of defining Show Screen Layout section in the Activity.  

  

Exporting Data with standard layout in Show Data screen  

Lastly, if no defined layout in Show Screen Layout field in General information and in Show Data screen, Standard default layout is used.   

  

  

  

In the exported data file, the default standard layout wherein all fields are displayed, used in the activity show data screen, will be the layout applied in the file.  

  

Just an additional information, if an Activity user (with data authorization limitation) will be exporting the data, based on the data authorization (Activity Field selection and Data Privilege), it will be applied on the data to be exported.  

Show Screen: Apply Activity Filters for every Activity layout  

In our latest update, we've added a feature to improve data filtering consistency in Show Screen. This enhancement is especially helpful for Process users who need to provide pre-filtered data to Activity users, ensuring that the data displayed aligns precisely with user-defined filters across multiple layouts.  

Now, by setting "Apply Field Selection on Show" to YES, any Activity Fields defined will apply uniformly across all Activity layouts for a seamless experience. Regardless of whether the layout is Standard or Additional (created by user); users will see the same filtered data, maintaining consistency throughout their work process. When users switch from one layout to another, the application will reapply the filter to ensure data consistency.  

 

Comments and Eventlogs Availability in Activity Show Data  

Comments are now available in Activity Show Data Screen only. You will not see comments pane in Modelling Show Screen. As an enhancement, added option to create and read comments in the object page of the record.  This makes it easier to collaborate and provide feedback in context, ensuring that communication is more focused and accessible where it’s most relevant.  

  

In addition, all event log functionalities are now available on the object page. This powerful update allows you to view and analyze historical changes, compare different versions of objects, and track modifications—all in one place. This provides deeper insights and a more comprehensive understanding of object evolution.  

 

Individual Log Entry upon import data (Update and Insert write mode)  

SAP PaPM Cloud Universal Model now offers record-based traceability for imported data. Each record will have a separate log entry that includes the Old Value and New Value when the Update and Insert write modes are used during the import process.  

The Old Value represents the value prior to the update, while the New Value signifies the value following the update.

 

You can easily view the Old and New Values by clicking on the event log icon for a record that has been updated and expanding the section labeled 'Changes.'  

Importing and exporting of data in xlsx file format 

With the new enhancement, excel file can now be used for importing of data. The excel files that can be used for importing the data may be introduced to the system via Manage Comments and via comments. 

The structure of the excel file should be the same on what we have for the csv files, it should have a header line in which the technical name is used as header columns.  This feature also supports importing of data with complex business objects, like the one which has hierarchy, in one go.  The enhancement only allows an excel file with a maximum of 64MB to be used as an import file or an error message will appear if the user tries to upload a file of more than 64MB. 

Excel files can be added via Manage Comments; 

 

as well as via Activity Show Data 

 

Importing Files can be done via Activities Screen; 

 

Or via Show Data Screen 

 

Complex business objects (Parent-Childs, Document-LineItems, etc.) are supported, if the entities data is provided in separate tabs of the excel file. They are imported in one go. 

 

Exporting of Data can be done on Show Data Screen 

 

Uploading an excel file with more than 64MB of data will return a Maximum file size error. 

REPORT 

Manage Reports: Show Report  

We have introduced an enhancement in Reports wherein if “Auto-Refresh Page” setting is enabled, the user will be able to see if another user makes any changes (such as adding text to the editor or insert of Show Element) in the same report page. This will ensure that users will always see the latest updates in real-time without the need to refresh the page.  

  

Just be aware that to balance performance and usability, the system will limit refresh to no more than once every 30 seconds. This prevents excessive refreshing while keeping the information up to date.  

Administration 

Manage Tenant Connections: AI Core Service  

Manage Tenant Connections has introduced a new Connection Target Type called "AI Core Service" which can be created to refer to a specific AI Core Service destination. This will allow communication between applications running in SAP BTP and SAP PaPM Cloud UM to be able to utilize Artificial Intelligence in the application. For more information on how to configure this, see Manage Tenant Connections: AI Core Service.  

As SAP PaPM Cloud UM continues to evolve, staying up to date with its latest advancements will ensure you maximize the value of your investment. We hope this overview has given you a clearer picture of the exciting possibilities that lie ahead. Be sure to explore and experiment with these features to fully leverage their potential within your organization! 

See you on the next blogpost! 😊

entry.title: Issue Management and Remediation with SAP Risk and Assurance Management
entry.content:
If any irregularities are found in your business data or processes, the system will trigger issue creation. 

These issues must be analyzed to determine if they are confirmed issues or false positives.

During the investigation and remediation phase, task lists with predefined tasks help you to process issues in a structural manner.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

If you want to learn more about Issue Management and Remediation in SAP Risk and Assurance Management, check out the detailed overview on the SAP Help Portal: Overall Picture - Issue Management and Remediation.

See also SAP Financial Compliance / Risk and Assurance Management covers much more than Finance

entry.title: Re: BAPI for posting FI document (FB01)
entry.content:
Call this BAPI 

  CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_ACC_DOCUMENT_POST'
        EXPORTING
          document header    = lw_document_header
        TABLES
          accountgl               = lt_accountgl
          accountreceivable = lt_accountcust
          accountpayable    = lt_accountvend
          accounttax        = lt_accounttax
          currencyamount    = lt_currencyamount
          return                     = lt_return
          extension2        = lt_extension2. 

DATA: lv_success TYPE abap_bool.
lv_success = abap_true.

LOOP AT lt_return INTO DATA(ls_return).
WRITE: / ls_return-message. "Output return message
IF ls_return-type = 'E' OR ls_return-type = 'A'.
lv_success = abap_false. "Set success flag to false on error
ENDIF.
ENDLOOP.

"Commit Work if Successful
IF lv_success = abap_true.
CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT'
EXPORTING
wait = 'X'.
WRITE: / 'Document posted successfully:', lv_document_number.
ELSE.
CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_TRANSACTION_ROLLBACK'. "Rollback on error
WRITE: / 'Document posting failed.'.
ENDIF.

entry.title: What's New in SAP Convergent Charging 2023 FPS02
entry.content:
New Release

PUBLIC

 November 2024

Dear Convergent Charging and BRIM community,

We are delighted to announce the latest release of SAP Convergent Charging 2023 FPS2. This update brings a range of exciting new capabilities designed to enhance functionality and user experience. Key highlights include the immediate availability of shared allowances, support for 16 decimal precision in pricing, and enhanced features within the cockpit to make it even more user-friendly.

Let's dive into these latest product features in detail, highlighting our latest pricing and charging capabilities as part of the SAP Billing and Revenue Innovation Management (BRIM) suite.

Immediate availability of shared allowances

We have observed an increasing trend in the utilization of shared allowance concepts among our B2B and B2C customers. The ability to manage shared resources effectively and immediately is therefore crucial, especially when businesses relying on usage-based billing. Shared allowances, such as pooled data or voice minutes, are a key component in B2B and B2C models where multiple users or services draw from a common pool. Traditionally, however, we have received feedback indicating a delay between the creation of a shared allowance and its availability for consumption, which has potentially impacted customer experience and operational efficiency.

With SAP Convergent Charging 2023 FPS2, we’re excited to introduce an enhancement in the rating process that makes shared allowances instantly available upon creation. Now, the allocation of these allowances across rating processes is atomic. This means if the distribution fails, the system immediately rolls back any changes, maintaining data integrity and returning a clear error for rapid troubleshooting.

Key Benefits:

  • Enhanced Orchestration: Improve orchestration of shared allowances from an external system.
  • Optimized B2B Support: Improve support for B2B use cases using shared allowances.

By enabling instant availability of shared allowances, businesses can now streamline their operations, reduce delay-related disruptions, and deliver a smoother experience to their customers. For more information about this new feature, refer to the Enabling the Immediate Availability of Shared Allowances topic on the SAP Help Portal.

Support for up to 16 decimal places

In today’s digital subscription economy, SAAS based operators require process millions of usage-based transactions where prices are very small. Example of such micro-transactions, such as API Gateway calls billed at rates as low as €0.0000015 (need for 7 decimal places) , require a high degree of accuracy to ensure fair and transparent billing.

To meet such requirements, SAP Convergent Charging introduces support for extended decimal precision with Increase decimal places from 6 to 16 for numbers that can be used in counters, parameter values, mapping/range table columns. However, depending on the implemented scenario, you need to check the capability of the Subscription Order Management and Convergent Invoicing component to support such high precision numbers. In case the supported precision of decimal is lower than the one supported by SAP Convergent Charging, decimal numbers shared with those components may lose precision which may cause some data inconsistencies.

Key Feature:

  • Change database data type from DECIMAL (28, 6) to DECIMAL (38, 16)
  • UI enhancements to manage 16 decimal places (Core Tool and Cockpit)
  • New WS version for catalog v4 to support 16 decimal places
  • Increase of maximum internal precision from 12 to 32

Feature enablement:

  • SAPInst in case of a new system
  • SAP Note 3471570 in case of an existing system (as of CC 2023 FPS2)
  • When enabled, there is no disablement procedure

Benefits

  • Improve support for new pricing models

Learn more about this feature from our SAP Note released for Migrating SAP Convergent Charging to support up to 16 decimal places

Introducing the New Fiori Apps for enhanced User Experience in the Cockpit

We remain dedicated to continuously improving the user experience of SAP CC by introducing new capabilities in the Fiori apps. In this release, we are excited to deliver few cornerstone features for SAP CC administrators and billing specialist to support key operational activities. Let’s dive deep into these latest Fiori app additions in the Cockpit.

1. Management of Users and Roles in the Cockpit

Earlier, administrators had to rely on the Core Tool to create and manage users and roles. However, now to streamline such key functionality, we have now introduced the 'Manage User Accounts' Fiori app. This Fiori app enables administrators to efficiently create, manage and assign required roles for individual and service user accounts directly from the Fiori App in Cockpit, offering a more intuitive and productive experience.

 

Benefits

  • Improve security management
  • Improved the user experience

Learn more about this feature from SAP Help portal: Manage User Accounts

 

2. SAP Fiori app to Display Subscriber Accounts in the Cockpit

Billing Specialist can now efficiently search and view the subscriber account, contract and their subscriber tables directly from the Fiori App. This empowers the billing specialists with an intuitive tool for account verification, of the provisioned subscribers in SAP CC.

 Benefits

  • Improve verification of provisioned subscriber account data in SAP Convergent Charging

Learn more about this feature from SAP Help portal: Display Subscriber Accounts

 

3. SAP Fiori app to Display Provider Contracts in the Cockpit

Billing specialists can now efficiently search for and view existing provider contracts and their associated contract items directly through the Fiori app. In an integrated SAP Solution scenario, this customer master data is created in the Subscription Order Management (SOM) application and then replicated into SAP CC.

This enhanced functionality enables seamless verification of provisioned provider contract data, ensuring accuracy and reliability in the rating process within SAP Convergent Charging.

 Benefits

  • Improve verification of provisioned provider contract data in SAP Convergent Charging

Learn more about this feature from SAP Help portal: Display Provider Contracts

 

4. Support for additional pricing components in the rate plan for the Cockpit

While using Fiori Apps for defining rate plan from cockpit, now pricing specialists will have access to additional pricing components.

  • Time-Slot-Based Addition Aggregator: to compute the sum of multiple amounts, each amount representing a period within or out of the specified recurring time slot.
    • New pricing component “Timeslot-Based Addition” in the pricing tree of charges and pricing functions​
    • Enhancement of the Output, Created Allowances, and Notifications sections in the Execution Test panel to support a high number of items​
  • Proration Operator: to enable proration of number property based on a specified recurring period
    • New pricing component “Proration” in the pricing tree of charges and pricing functions

Learn more about additional pricing components feature from SAP Help portal: Time-Slot-Based Addition & Proration

Other Enhancements

In addition to the features mentioned above, SAP Convergent Charging 2023 FPS02 also introduces other enhancements:

  • New Web service operations for integration with SAP CPQ

In our latest release, we've enabled the integration between SAP Convergent Charging (SAP CC) and SAP Configure Price Quote (SAP CPQ) to support usage-based integration scenarios. You will be now able to support integration scenarios such as:

  • Usage with no recurring fees
  • Usage as part of a subscription with recurring fees
  • Subscription service with mixed recurring & usage charges

These new enhancements introduce seamless import and export capabilities for data within catalog/agreement mapping tables and range tables, leveraging new SOAP web service operations.

Additionally, we have upgraded two existing SOAP web service operations—mappingTableFind and rangeTableFind- to include two new elements:

totalRowCount: Provides the total number of rows in a table.

objectChangeNumber: Tracks changes made to the table, improving data visibility and traceability. (e.g. in order to identify whether a table has been modified or not after a quote has been accepted by the customer)

Benefits

  • Improve the integration of SAP Convergent Charging with SAP Configure, Price, and Quote (SAP CPQ)

 

  • Decreasing the retention period for counter snapshots

Effective data retention policies are vital for maintaining optimal system performance and resource efficiency. Take the example of the COUNTER_SNAPSHOT table used in rerating processes—it can grow excessively, consuming significant disk storage and memory, which negatively impacts system performance and the efficiency of rating operations.

We’ve received feedback from customers indicating that their configured data retention periods are often unnecessarily long compared to their actual business needs, leading to inefficient use of database resources. To address this, reducing the history of counter snapshots stored in the database can help optimize resource usage.

To support such scenarios, we’ve introduced best-practice guidelines in our latest SAP Note - 3518679, detailing the steps to effectively adjust the retention period for counter snapshots.: https://me.sap.com/notes/3518679

 

  • 3rd party updates

With this release, we’ve upgraded to Spring Boot 3.2, ensuring compatibility with the latest advancements in third-party libraries, improved security, and enhanced runtime efficiency.

More Information

SAP Convergent Charging 2023 FPS 02 has been released and is generally available to customers from October 2023 and is available on the SAP Software Download Center.

For more information on SAP Convergent Charging 2023 FPS02, check out the following links:

  • What’s New in 2023 FPS02
  • Delivery and Information Note for SAP Convergent Charging 2023 FPS 02
  • Product Page on SAP Help Portal
  • Questions and Answers for SAP Convergent Charging in SAP Community

entry.title: Is it possible to integrate two different SuccessFactors instances? Not in same landscape!
entry.content:
Hello Dear Members,

I would need your inputs on this question.

My customer has a requirement to integrate two different SuccessFactors landscapes, where data for one legal entity needs to be transferred from the Parent Instance to the Child Instance via SAP Cloud Platform Integration (CPI) or any other possible way.

  • Is this integration technically feasible?
  • If yes, what challenges might we face during implementation?
  • If not, are there any recommended workarounds?

Additionally, can the integration include all data (e.g., employee master data, payroll-related information, organizational structure, etc.), or is it limited to transferring only a Mini Master Data (such as Employee Profile)?

Looking forward to insights from the community on best practices or possible limitations for such an integration.

Best regards,

Siva

entry.title: Empower your frontline workers with SAP Service and Asset Manager and Samsung Knox Capture
entry.content:
In today’s fast-paced business environment, efficiency and data accuracy are essential. For organizations managing assets, from manufacturing plants to critical infrastructure, mobile enterprise asset management (EAM) solutions have become essential tools as they enable real-time access to asset information and work orders and allow technicians to perform maintenance and repairs more efficiently. 

Integrating scanning functions into these mobile solutions amplifies their impact, streamlining processes with smart scanning and an intuitive data capture. Scanning enables instant, accurate access to data on the go by reducing manual data entry, minimizing errors, and accelerating workflows. What are the main use cases that our mobile team has identified for scanning?  

  1. Rapid Identification and Tracking of Assets 
    Scanning features allow technicians to instantly access an asset’s data by simply scanning a barcode or QR code. This immediate access can include assets, spare parts, maintenance work orders and notifications. For organizations with large inventories, scanning technology eliminates the need for manual searches and ensures quick, accurate identification of stock. 
  2. Improved Data Accuracy 
    Manual data entry is both time-consuming and prone to error. Scanning eliminates the risk of transcription errors, helping to keep data records accurate ultimately minimizing administrative overhead and ensuring the reliability of asset and inventory data.

SAP Service and Asset Manager and Samsung Knox Capture  

As announced in one of our previous blog posts (Navigating the speed bumps across the supply chain) that introduced SAP and Samsung partnership motivation and areas of collaboration, we are integrating our mobile flagship application SAP Service and Asset Manager with Samsung Knox Capture as part of the 2410 release.  

This integration will help enable the user to scan barcodes and automate filling of input fields during maintenance and inventory activities. The programmable Hardware Key on Samsung Galaxy rugged devices will also enable 1 click trigger of scanning functionality, allowing users to optimize their daily activity by enabling them to scan single or multi barcodes simultaneously based on their use case needs with the simple press of a hardware key.  Details here: SAP Service and Asset Manager and Samsung Knox capture integration 

Why Samsung Knox for an integrated data capture? 

  • Smart scanning: Faster camera boot-up, accurate auto-focus and tight Samsung hardware integration 
  • Intuitive data capture: Single, selective, or all-at-once batch scanning from integrated in SAP Service and Asset Manager maintenance and inventory mobile workflows. Hardware key to trigger scanning, or keyboard wedge 
  • Effortless deployment: Tailored for SAP Service and Asset Manager SDK and provided as part of the out of the box feature set when customer choses Samsung devices. 

Conclusion 

The integration of scanning functionalities into mobile enterprise asset management apps represents a significant advancement in technology. By improving accuracy, efficiency, and compliance, scanning tools help companies streamline their operations, reduce costs, and empower their workforce. SAP Service and Asset Manager and Samsung Knox integration helps organizations with business ready capabilities that combines the software and hardware strengths of SAP and Samsung. 

#SAPServiceandAssetManager #SamsungKnoxCapture

entry.title: Parallel insert requests in CAP
entry.content:
Hi,

I have to update around 4Mio records in a flat CAP Table.

My first aproach was to run n-batches of inserts with around 10.000 Records each Insert.

This took around 1hour and crashes with a timeout.

My next approach was, to run batches in parallel.

for (var p = 0; p < promisesCount; p++) {
                        var promises = [];
                        startCounter = counter;

                        for (var b = 0; b < processSize; b++) {
                            var records = batch_util.createBatch(rows, counter, batchSize);
                            if (records.length > 0) {
                                promises.push(INSERT.into(CustomerOrderHistory).entries(records));
                            }
                            counter = counter + batchSize;
                        } // loop batch
                        endCounter = counter;
                        await Promise.all(promises);

                        console.log(`Inserting data from ${startCounter} to ${endCounter}`);

                    } // loop promises

 

When I run the code locally against my Postgres Docker image it runs in around 7 Minutes.

Deployed on the BTP against a Postgress instance I take at least 30min and crashes at around 1.5mio Records

It seems, that on the BTP the  request are running seqnetially and not parallel.

 

Any suggestions?
Jens

entry.title: Re: How To Combine SAP GUI Scripting And Selenium WebDriver
entry.content:
Hi @Stefan-Schnell 

I have set ElementVisualizationMode to true, and the GUI elements are highlighting with a red boundary as expected. However, the Hit event is not being triggered. Below is the code I am using. Could you please review it and let me know if I am missing something?

Thank you in advance for your assistance.

Best regards,
K Girish Kumar

 

using System;
using System.Runtime.InteropServices;
using Interop.SAPFEWSELib;

class SAPGuiRecorder
{
    static void Main(string[] args)
    {
        try
        {
            // Initialize SAP GUI scripting engine
            Console.WriteLine("Initializing SAP GUI scripting...");
            var sapGui = InitializeSAPGui();
            if (sapGui == null)
            {
                Console.WriteLine("SAP GUI scripting engine not found.");
                return;
            }

            // Get the first connection and session
            var session = GetSAPSession(sapGui);
            if (session == null)
            {
                Console.WriteLine("No active session found.");
                return;
            }

            // Enable visualization mode and subscribe to the Hit event
            session.ActiveWindow.ElementVisualizationMode = true;
            Console.WriteLine("Visualization Mode Enabled.");
            session.Hit += OnHitEvent;

            Console.WriteLine("SAP GUI recorder is running. Interact with the GUI to trigger events. Press Enter to exit.");
            Console.ReadLine();

            // Clean up resources
            FreeCOMObject(session);
            FreeCOMObject(sapGui);
        }
        catch (Exception ex)
        {
            Console.WriteLine($"Error: {ex.Message}");
        }
    }

    // Initialize SAP GUI scripting engine
    static GuiApplication InitializeSAPGui()
    {
        object sapGuiAuto = Marshal.BindToMoniker("SAPGUI");
        return sapGuiAuto != null ? (GuiApplication)InvokeMethod(sapGuiAuto, "GetScriptingEngine") : null;
    }

    // Get the active SAP session
    static GuiSession GetSAPSession(GuiApplication app)
    {
        try
        {
            var connection = (GuiConnection)app.Connections.Item(0);
            return (GuiSession)connection.Children.Item(0);
        }
        catch
        {
            return null;
        }
    }

    // Event handler for the Hit event
    private static void OnHitEvent(GuiSession session, GuiComponent component, string innerObject)
    {
        Console.WriteLine("\n--- Hit Event Detected ---");
        Console.WriteLine($"Session ID: {session.Id}");
        Console.WriteLine($"Component Name: {component.Name}");
        Console.WriteLine($"Inner Object: {innerObject}");
    }

    // Helper: Invoke a method via reflection
    static dynamic InvokeMethod(object obj, string methodName, object[] methodParams = null)
    {
        return obj.GetType().InvokeMember(methodName, System.Reflection.BindingFlags.InvokeMethod, null, obj, methodParams);
    }

    // Helper: Free COM object
    static void FreeCOMObject(object obj)
    {
        if (obj != null)
        {
            Marshal.ReleaseComObject(obj);
        }
    }
}

entry.title: Re: Integration Suite, Basic Edition - Restricted No. of...
entry.content:
Hi @Kareem-Hany ,

without knowing the specific business context of the integration and messaging rationale here are some general recommendations to minimize message volume. But please note my last point below:

  1. Implement batch processing for bulk data transfers
  2. Use event-based integrations instead of polling
  3. Apply efficient data filtering at source system (Only process / message data you absolutely have to)
  4. Compress payloads where possible (ZIP, tar, etc.)
  5. Design granular, purposeful message mappings

As for message limit management:

  • Standard Allocation: 10,000 messages per period
  • Overage: Automatically provisioned in 10,000 message blocks
  • Billing Monitoring Recommendations:
    • Monitor message consumption through the SAP BTP Integration Suite dashboard
    • Set up alerts at 70-80% message limit
    • Forecast integration message volume quarterly
    • Budget for potential additional message blocks
    • Consider pre-purchasing extra message blocks if consistent overages anticipated

Here are some further technical considerations:

  • Track message types (inbound/outbound)
  • Review integration design for (continuous) optimization
  • Implement message throttling mechanisms
  • Align integration strategy with actual business process requirements

This approach might help your customer proactively manage integration message consumption and associated costs in SAP BTP's Integration Suite. 

However, these ar all technical, cost-oriented considerations. There might be an overarching business rationale for the underlying integrated business process to begin with which might call for more messages to be welcomed: Opportunity cost! If the cost of throttling means lost business (opportunity) the value of which is higher than the savings from not having the expenditure for another 10K of messages, throttling hampers business in lieu of benefitting it. This consideration should be applied to the cost of monitoring and all cost of measures taken to avoid the expenditure for additional messages.

entry.title: Bulk Migration Tool to Convert Classic Assets to the Optimized Experience
entry.content:
In a recent Blog Post, the SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) Team reported that the Classic Experience (Stories and Analytic Applications) and other non-strategic features will be replaced with the Optimized Experience as part of their ongoing improvement initiatives.

Customer feedback had indicated the need for assistance during this transition. With this, the 2024 Q4 QRC release of SAP Analytics Cloud will support a Bulk Migration Tool.

⚠️  NOTE it is limited to Enterprise SAP Analytics Cloud. It is being enhanced to be supported within Embedded SAP Analytics Cloud. It is recommended to monitor the Roadmap Explorer for SAP Analytics Cloud (HERE) for the latest status.

Let’s explore some of the key features of the Bulk Migration Tool and how it can facilitate your transition to the Optimized Experience.

 

Bulk Migration Tool (Story Conversion)

The Bulk Migration Tool is accessible via the Stories Module under the Story Conversion Tab. There are a few steps involved to convert an asset to the Optimized Experience. It includes:

  1. Selection of Assets
  2. Conversion Check & Status
  3. Conversion Settings
  4. Conversion Summary

Selection of Assets

Within the Story Conversion Tab, there is a list of assets that the corresponding user has Edit Access to. For the initial release, it will be limited Classic Stories OR Classic Stories within Optimized View Mode. It means that for Classic Analytic Applications, Designers will still have to convert on a per object basis.

⚠️  NOTE the Bulk Migration Tool is being enhanced to support the conversion of Classic Analytic Applications to Optimized Experience. It is recommended to monitor the Roadmap Explorer for SAP Analytics Cloud (HERE) for the latest status.

Designers can either select the object directly OR select a folder. Based on the selection, the counter for the number of Stories Selected will increase.

⚠️  NOTE there is a maximum of 50 Stories which can be selected at one time.

⚠️  NOTE with the selection of a Folder, it ignores unsupported assets. Furthermore, it does not support Stories within a subfolder.

Once the Designer has selected the objects that need to be converted to the Optimized Experience, the Designer should click on the blue button called Convert to Optimized Story.

Conversion Check & Status

Before the Bulk Migration, there is a conversion process that takes place. There are three possible statuses that can appear which is based on the unsupported features HERE:

  1. Ready to Convert
  2. Feature Limitation
  3. Blocked

Ready to Convert

These are objects where the Bulk Migration Tool has detected no unsupported OR blocking features. It means that there is nothing required by the Designer to proceed with the migration.

Feature Limitation

These are objects where the Bulk Migration Tool has detected at least one unsupported feature. From a Best Practice perspective, it depends on the limitations whether it should be addressed before the migration or after.

For example, for features that result in a Ghost Widget (i.e. Error Bars, Geographical Visualization Feature Layer, and so on), it is recommended to deal with these before the migration. It is because it prevents the need for the Designer to have to re-create the entire widget.

⚠️  NOTE there are certain features (i.e. Clock Widget) where there is no direct replacement and does involve having to re-create the object or use an alternative. For these features, it is recommended for the Designer to not touch these before migration.

For features that have a seamless transition or are silently removed (i.e. Grouping, Display Full Range Slider, and so on), it is recommended that the Designer does not touch these objects prior to migration.

Blocked

These are objects where the Bulk Migration Tool has detected at least one blocking feature. It means that the Designer must deal with these unsupported features before migration.

However, the option to click on “Next” does not remain disabled as the other selected objects will proceed with the migration.

Objects with Feature Limitation or Blocked

For objects that have an unsupported or blocking feature, there is an information icon that is available. It will include the same information that is available via the manual conversion to the Optimized Experience.

There is an additional option to launch the object in a New Tab to address the impacted features.

⚠️  NOTE for objects that have the unsupported or feature limitation addressed, the Designer is required to cancel and reselect the objects for conversion.

Conversion Settings

With the Conversion Settings there is an option to either convert a copy of the original OR convert the original object to the Optimized Experience. For the option to convert a copy of the original, it will default to the same folder as the original object. There is an option to create a brand-new folder to store these converted stories.

From a Best Practice perspective, it is recommended to use the Overwrite Classic Stories option as it ensures that the original object is converted to the Optimized Experience. It is because with a Save Converted Stories as Copies, it creates a brand-new object and does not retain share privileges, bookmarks, and so on.

⚠️  NOTE the conversion from Classic to Optimized Experience is non-reversible. It means that once an object has been converted, there is no option to revert it back to the Classic Experience.

Conversion & Conversion Summary

With the Conversion Process, there is a progress bar that is shown that gives the Designer an overview on the status. There is an option to cancel the conversion at any point in time.

Based on when the cancel button is clicked, there could be some objects that have completed the conversion. The Confirm Cancellation dialog will indicate to the Designer which objects have been completed and how many were remaining which can be cancelled.

Upon the completion of the Bulk Migration, there is an overview which is provided to the Designer. There is an option to download the status as an XLS for auditing reasons.

⚠️  NOTE Stories with Model Variables will not be prompted at the time of Story Conversion. Instead, the values that had been set will be retained.

 

Steps to Use the Bulk Migration Tool

Preparation

It is recommended that Designers familiarize themselves with the Restrictions of the Optimized Experience before initiating the Bulk Migration. It is because it will help them use the tool effectively and make an informed decision in terms of which objects can be converted even though there might be some unsupported features.

Post-Migration Review

It is important that once a migration has been completed, to review the asset. For assets that fail the conversion process, it is important to review the story, and attempt a manual conversion.

 

Conclusion

The transition from the Classic Design Experience to the Optimized Design Experience in SAP Analytics Cloud marks a significant step forward in enhancing the platform's capabilities.

The Bulk Migration Tool plays a vital role in facilitating this transition. By following the Best Practices and leveraging the Bulk Migration Tool, Designers can ensure a quick transition and unlock the full potential of the Optimized Experience.

entry.title: Re: U.S Tax Reporter Year End 2024
entry.content:
We have applied support pack level 138 and thus both notes 3470796 and 3511561.

Per irs.gov, the super catch up "applies for individuals who attain age 60, 61, 62, or 63 in 2025 ".

The configuration of the maximum age = 63 as delivered in view V_T7USBEN03 does not work, as it does allow the extra $3750 catch up for those employees who are turning 64 in 2025.

The $3750 is determined by calculating the $7500 catch up limit (401CL) by 50%. The total Super catch up amount therefore is 11,250 (higher than constant 401HC 10,000)

entry.title: Unmanaged late numbering - ADJUST_NUMBER in save phase
entry.content:
Hello All,

I usually use unmanaged numbering in managed RAP application.

When I do this, when I create a new entity, then in adjust_number method, I have the entity I just created :

 

However, in the unmanaged RAP application, MAPPED is empty :

This is the create by association method I'm using :

  METHOD cba_Orders.
    DATA n TYPE i VALUE 1.

    LOOP AT entities_cba INTO DATA(ls_entity).
    LOOP AT ls_entity-%target INTO DATA(ls_target).

      APPEND VALUE #(
       pid = n
       product_id = ls_target-product_id
       quantity = ls_target-quantity
      ) TO lhc_ZTEST_CUST_NORTHWIND=>mt_buffer_create.

      APPEND VALUE #(
      %cid = ls_target-%cid
      %pid = n
      product_id = ls_target-product_id ) TO mapped-ztest_cust_northwind_order.

      n = n + 1.
    ENDLOOP.
  ENDLOOP.

ENDMETHOD.

Am I doing something wrong ?

 

For now, I'm using a workaround : I store the PID in a buffer table with data created, and I use this buffer table to loop to PID generated in CREATE BY ASSOCIATION and use it in the ADJUST_NUMBER methode :

METHOD adjust_numbers.

  LOOP AT lhc_ZTEST_CUST_NORTHWIND=>mt_buffer_create ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_buffer>).

    SELECT MAX( order_id )
      FROM ztest_cust_northwind_order
      WHERE product_id = @<ls_buffer>-product_id
      INTO @DATA(lv_orderid).

      lv_orderid = lv_orderid + 1.

      APPEND VALUE #(
      %pid = <ls_buffer>-pid
      %tmp = VALUE #( product_id = <ls_buffer>-product_id )
      product_id = <ls_buffer>-product_id
      order_id = lv_orderid ) TO mapped-ztest_cust_northwind_order.

      <ls_buffer>-order_id = lv_orderid.

    ENDLOOP.

  ENDMETHOD.

But I don't understand why it is not coming natively in MAPPED structure ?

 

Thx in advance

entry.title: My Journey Through Devtoberfest 2024: Learning, Connecting, and Growing with SAP
entry.content:
English Version.

After returning from my September vacation, I started seeing LinkedIn posts about Devtoberfest 2024. Although I’d heard about it in previous years, I had never taken the time to look into whether it could actually help me in the future.

After reading one of those posts, I decided to register and experience it firsthand to see what it was all about, what activities it offered, and how it could benefit my future in SAP.

So, I signed up and prepared myself for the four weeks of Devtoberfest, with the goal of exploring how far I could go. During the event, there was a break for the Teched online, another key week for learning about SAP's future.

Each day of the week was dedicated to a specific topic:

  • Monday: ABAP & CAP
  • Tuesday: Tooling
  • Wednesday: Integration
  • Thursday: MAD (Machine Learning, AI, and Data)
  • Friday: Frontend

On September 23, the activities for the first week began. Although I wasn’t entirely sure what the event or the activity dynamics would be like, I was determined to seize the opportunity and learn about the latest SAP innovations.

Before each week ended, the activities for the following week would be unlocked, with links provided for easy access to each one. There were also video sessions featuring SAP experts sharing their knowledge, followed by questions to reinforce learning. However, what I enjoyed most were the Friday activities, which were designed to foster community interaction: sharing photos of our pets, our workspaces, or finding bugs in different languages, and even uploading Halloween-themed photos.

In conclusion, I think anyone in the field of SAP development should participate in Devtoberfest at least once. It’s a very well-organized event with a lot to learn. Personally, I discovered tools like SAP BUILD, which allows the creation of applications, workflows, and forms without needing coding skills — the trendy Low-code No-code approach. I also learned about Clean Core, ABAP for Cloud, Integration Suite, and Fiori for the frontend. These four weeks not only helped me improve my skills but also allowed me to connect with the community and meet SAP specialists.

A big thank you to @thomas_jung  for organizing the event, to @ajmaradiaga for his posts that motivated me to participate in Devtoberfest 2024, and to all the experts who shared their knowledge.

I finished the event with a score of 45,710 points, and now I eagerly await to see if I'll be one of the lucky ones to receive a special prize!

Spanish Version.

Después de las vacaciones de septiembre, empecé a ver publicaciones en LinkedIn sobre el Devtoberfest 2024. Aunque había oído hablar de él en años anteriores, nunca me había detenido a investigar si realmente podría ayudarme en el futuro.

Al leer una de esas publicaciones, decidí inscribirme y experimentarlo desde dentro para ver en qué consistía, qué actividades ofrecía y cómo podría beneficiarme en mi futuro en SAP.

Así que me apunté y me preparé para las cuatro semanas del Devtoberfest, con la intención de explorar hasta dónde podría llegar. Durante el evento, hubo una pausa para el Teched online, otra semana clave para conocer el futuro de SAP.

Cada día de la semana estaba dedicado a un tema específico:

  • Lunes: ABAP & CAP
  • Martes: Tooling
  • Miércoles: Integration
  • Jueves: MAD (Machine Learning, AI and Data)
  • Viernes: Frontend

El 23 de septiembre comenzaron las actividades de la primera semana. Aunque no tenía claro cómo sería el evento ni las dinámicas de las actividades, estaba decidido a aprovechar la oportunidad y aprender las novedades que SAP ofrece.

Antes de finalizar cada semana, se activaban las actividades de la siguiente, proporcionando enlaces para acceder a cada una sin problemas. También había sesiones en video con expertos de SAP que compartían su conocimiento, seguidas de preguntas para reforzar el aprendizaje. Sin embargo, lo que más disfruté fueron las actividades de los viernes, diseñadas para fomentar la interacción en la comunidad: compartir fotos con nuestras mascotas, de nuestros espacios de trabajo o buscar errores en diferentes lenguajes, y hasta subir fotos temáticas de Halloween.

En conclusión, creo que cualquier persona en el ámbito de desarrollo en SAP debería participar al menos una vez en el Devtoberfest. Es un evento muy bien organizado donde se aprende muchísimo. Yo, personalmente, descubrí herramientas como SAP BUILD, que permite crear aplicaciones, flujos de trabajo y formularios sin necesidad de programación, el Low-code No code de moda. Además, aprendí sobre el Clean Core, ABAP para Cloud, Integration Suite, y Fiori para el frontend. Estas cuatro semanas me permitieron no solo mejorar mis habilidades, sino también conectar con la comunidad y conocer a especialistas de SAP.

Gracias a @thomas_jung  por la organización del evento, a @ajmaradiaga  por sus publicaciones que me motivaron a participar en Devtoberfest 2024, y a todos los expertos que compartieron su conocimiento.

Finalicé el evento con una puntuación de 45,710 puntos, ¡y ahora espero con emoción si seré uno de los afortunados en recibir un premio especial!

entry.title: The Risks and Rewards of Using PFCGMASSCOLLASSIGN in SAP Security; Mass Composite Role Updates
entry.content:
This post isn’t about explaining how to use PFCGMASSCOLLASSIGN—you can easily figure that out. Instead, it’s focused on highlighting the critical importance of such mass maintenance T-codes and the potential risks and issues that can arise when using them.

The PFCGMASSCOLLASSIGN t-code is incredibly useful for handling mass updates to composite roles. However, it also comes with significant risks, making it advisable for use only by experienced professionals. In this post, I’ll cover both how to use it effectively and the key precautions to keep in mind.

PFCGMASSCOLLASSIGN-

This tcode is used to add/delete one/more single role(s) from one/more composite role(s).

It’s as straightforward as it sounds: enter the composite roles you want to edit, specify the single roles you wish to add or select, and then choose the appropriate action (Add/Delete) accordingly -

Here I am adding ZSINGLE2 role to ZCPMPOSITE1 to ZCOMPOSITE4 (which already have ZSINGLE1 role).

I probably don’t need to explain this, but I’ll still provide a brief one-liner description of the execution modes-

  • Executing with Simulation mode will not make any changes and will tell what all changes be performed.
  • Executing with Direct Execution mode will directly make the change.
  • Executing with Execute with prior simulation will simulate the changes and we then can execute the change. (Recommended). When executed with this option, below screen appears -

 

The system indicates the changes to be made using "+" and "-" signs. In this case, it shows the addition of new single roles to the composite roles. You can exclude roles from the list if needed, and upon clicking Execute, the changes will be applied as simulated. Whether you choose Direct Execution or Execute with Prior Simulation, you will be prompted to transport the updated roles once the execution is complete.

Now what could Go Wrong! -

The potential risk is human error. For instance, consider a newbie in SAP security experimenting with sensitive T-codes in the development system.

If Executed Add without filling any single / composite role -  It will add all singles to all composites (including SAP standard single/composite roles). When I executed in actual development system, it was making almost 800k updates (Simulation mode 😁)

If Executed Delete without filling any single / composite role - It will delete all single roles from all composite roles.

So, if it is ever executed in dev system, we will eventually end up having all this transported to Prod system. 

That's it, let me know in comments, if you have any questions.

Bonus Tip - We all love to maintain change definitions in Role Text, we can edit that, mentioning the change / requester details by clicking on Supplement Long Text in the tcode window.
